_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates batch, districts 68-72 (state tier). Hodges (R-68), Downey (D-69), Simonds (D-70), Anderson (D-71), Ware (R-72). Evidence-first finalization from primary roll calls (official VA LIS member-vote API / FastDemocracy LIS mirror), 2025/2026 second-reference constitutional-amendment floor votes (HJ1 reproductive freedom, HJ3 marriage, HJ2 voting rights), chief-patron bills, and issue scorecards. Downey and Anderson are 2026 freshmen flipped seats sworn Jan 14, 2026 -> scored ONLY on their documented strict-party-line opening-day amendment votes; all other cells null (no roll-call record yet, campaign positions left unscored). NEVER inferred from party where no recorded vote exists.

profile.religion: Methodist (per Ballotpedia biographical infobox; specific congregation not publicly documented)
profile.background: Myron Keith Hodges (R), VA House of Delegates District 68 (Middle Peninsula: Essex, Gloucester part, King & Queen, King William, Mathews, Middlesex). In office since 2011/2012 (formerly the old 98th District before 2021 redistricting); re-elected Nov 4, 2025 with 67.6%. Pharmacist (B.S., MCV School of Pharmacy, 1989). Serves on Health, Welfare & Institutions and General Laws.

keith-hodges.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-68 (sitting since 2011/2012; longtime member). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent conservative HARD record from official VA LIS member-vote API (MemberID 180), 2025 & 2026 Regular Sessions. SANCTITY: voted NO on both references of the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1 2025 first ref 51-48; HJ1/SJ1 2026 second ref) and NO on contraception-right bills (HB6, SB596) -> consistent pro-life voting. MARRIAGE: voted NO on the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment at every stage (HJ9/SJ249 2025; HJ3/SJ3/SB311 2026) and did NOT cross over when ~15% of House Republicans did on HJ3 (final 67-31) -> affirms man-woman marriage. FAMILY: YES on SB778 (obscene material to a minor; penalty, 93-6). ELECTION: NO on absentee-ballot cure/extension expansions (HB773/HB774/HB82/HB968) and on the felon-voting-rights amendment HJ2. PUBLIC JUSTICE: YES on enhanced fentanyl/drug + assault-on-correctional-officer + sexual-extortion penalties; NO on bail-leniency, earned-sentence-credit, and parole-expansion bills. IMMIGRATION: YES on HB77 (state/local LE federal cooperation), NO on HB1441/SB783 (bills restricting local-federal immigration cooperation) -> opposes sanctuary limits. SELF-DEFENSE: voted NO on the entire gun-control slate both sessions (assault-weapons & magazine bans, red-flag/substantial-risk orders, safe-storage, waiting period, background-check expansions). No rubric-relevant cross-party deviations. Federal-overreach, CBDC/balanced-budget, and a dedicated photo-ID bill returned NO EVIDENCE this window and were left null.

0.note: Voted NO on both references of the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1 2025 first ref 51-48; HJ1/SJ1 2026 second ref) and NO on contraception-right bills HB6 (65-32) and SB596 (65-33) — consistent pro-life voting record.

Voted NO at every stage on the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment (HJ9/SJ249 2025; HJ3/SJ3/SB311 2026); did NOT cross over when ~15% of House Republicans voted YES on HJ3 (final 67-31) — affirms marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted NO on codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3 second reference) — opposes same-sex marriage in law.

3.note: Voted YES on SB778 (2026), 'display of obscene material to a minor unlawful; penalty' (House 93-6) — supports criminal penalties for sexualized content shown to minors.

Voted NO on the 2026 absentee-ballot expansion bills — cure-process expansion (HB773/HB774), receipt-deadline extension (HB82), and machine-processing (HB968) — opposing expansion of mass mail-in/absentee voting.

1.note: Voted NO on bail/sentencing-leniency bills: release on unsecured bond (HB357), earned sentence credits while awaiting trial (HB361), and Parole Board juvenile-offender expansion (HB318/SB60) — opposes soft-on-crime policy.

2.note: Voted YES on enhanced penalties — Schedule I drug additions (HB558), novel-drug penalties (HB1103/SB106), sexual extortion (HB629), and assault on correctional officers (HB294/HB295) — proportional punishment of evildoers.
Voted YES on HB77 (2026, state/local LE federal cooperation, 90-7) and NO on HB1441/SB783 (2026), which would restrict local-federal immigration cooperation — opposes sanctuary policies.

1.note: Voted NO on the entire 2025-2026 gun-control slate: assault-firearms & magazine bans (HB217, HB1607/SB1181), red-flag/substantial-risk orders (HB1096/HB896/HB901/SB495), safe-storage mandates, 5-day waiting period (HB2631/SB891), and purchase-age restrictions — opposes red-flag laws, magazine limits, and 'assault weapons' bans.

profile.religion: Lutheran (per official VA House member profile)
profile.background: Mark C. Downey (D), VA House of Delegates District 69 (parts of Gloucester, James City, York counties + part of Newport News). Freshman — flipped the seat from GOP incumbent Chad Green in Nov 2025; sworn in Jan 14, 2026. Pediatrician (~25 years, Williamsburg area; the only physician in the General Assembly). B.S. Chemical Engineering, UVA (1988); M.D., VCU (1996). Serves on Counties, Cities & Towns and Health & Human Services.

mark-downey.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-69 (FRESHMAN, sworn Jan 14, 2026 after flipping the seat). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Scored ONLY on his documented strict-party-line opening-day second-reference constitutional-amendment floor votes; he has no other roll-call record yet, so every other cell is null (campaign-trail and advocacy-guide characterizations were NOT scored). MARRIAGE: HJ3 (marriage repeal amendment, second reference) passed the House 67-31 on Jan 14, 2026 with all 64 Democrats voting YES -> as a seated Democrat he is within that bloc. SANCTITY: HJ1 (reproductive-freedom amendment, second reference) passed 64-34 with all 64 Democrats voting YES. Individual line-item not separately pulled from the JS-rendered LIS roll-call page, but these were strict-party-line recorded floor votes (every House Democrat YES). His occupation is medicine; top stated priority is universal health coverage. Treat as a thin but real evidence record.

0.note: HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (second reference), passed the House 64-34 on Jan 14, 2026 with all 64 Democrats voting YES; Downey, a seated Democrat, is within that strict-party-line bloc — does not affirm personhood from conception.

HJ3, the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment (second reference), passed the House 67-31 on Jan 14, 2026 with all 64 Democrats voting YES; Downey is within that strict-party-line bloc — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Within the all-Democrat YES bloc codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3 second reference).

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND (no personal religious/church affiliation publicly documented)
profile.background: Shelly A. Simonds (D), VA House of Delegates District 70 (Newport News). In the House since 2020 (HD-94, famous 2017 tied-race loss; moved to HD-70 after 2021 redistricting); re-elected Nov 4, 2025. Vice Chair, House Education Committee. Educator; former Newport News School Board member. B.A. Bucknell; M.A. Stanford.

shelly-simonds.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-70 (sitting since 2020). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. SANCTITY: confirmed individual YEA on HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House 64-34, Jan 14, 2026; FastDemocracy LIS mirror); endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA and listed by REPRO Rising. MARRIAGE: HJ3 (marriage repeal amendment) passed the House 67-31 the same day with the full Democratic bloc YES (her individual HJ3 line not separately pulled, but her confirmed same-day HJ1 YEA places her in the bloc); documented opposition to a transgender-student 'forced outing' / parental-notification bill (Virginia Mercury). SELF-DEFENSE (strongest record): confirmed individual YEA on HB217 (assault-firearms & large-capacity-magazine ban) and HB901 (red-flag / substantial-risk-order expansion); Giffords-, Everytown-, and Brady-endorsed. iVoterGuide rates her 'Verified Liberal (Grade 10).' No cross-party deviations found. Public-justice, election-integrity, economic, immigration, and federal cells returned no clean Simonds-specific roll call and were left null (her $36M Safer Communities gun-violence-prevention budget item is community-based, not a soft-on-crime or anti-police vote).

0.note: Confirmed individual YEA on HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House 64-34, Jan 14, 2026) — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted for the reproductive-freedom amendment and is endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA / listed by REPRO Rising — opposite of abortion-abolition.

0.note: HJ3 (marriage repeal amendment) passed the House 67-31 on Jan 14, 2026 with the full Democratic bloc YES; her confirmed same-day HJ1 YEA places her in that bloc — does not affirm man-woman marriage. (Individual HJ3 line not separately pulled.)

1.note: Within the Democratic bloc codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3 second reference).

2.note: Documented opposition to a transgender-student 'forced outing' / parental-notification bill — does not reject transgender ideology.

1.note: Confirmed individual YEA on HB217 (assault-firearms & large-capacity-magazine ban, Feb 5 2026) and HB901 (red-flag / substantial-risk-order expansion, Feb 17 2026); Giffords/Everytown/Brady endorsed — supports red-flag laws, magazine limits, and assault-weapons bans.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND (no personal religious/church affiliation publicly documented)
profile.background: Jessica L. Anderson (D), VA House of Delegates District 71 (James City part, New Kent part, City of Williamsburg). Freshman — flipped the seat from GOP incumbent Amanda Batten in a Nov 2025 rematch (~52.7%); sworn in Jan 14, 2026. Lifelong Virginian; worked in Williamsburg-area public schools. A.S. business administration, Thomas Nelson CC. Serves on Counties, Cities & Towns; Public Safety; Education.

jessica-anderson.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-71 (FRESHMAN, sworn Jan 14, 2026 after flipping the seat). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Scored ONLY on her documented strict-party-line opening-day second-reference constitutional-amendment floor votes; no other roll-call record yet, so every other cell is null (2025 campaign positions were NOT scored). SANCTITY: HJ1 (reproductive-freedom amendment, second reference) passed the House 64-34 on Jan 14, 2026 with all 64 Democrats YES -> she is within that bloc. MARRIAGE: HJ3 (marriage repeal amendment) passed 67-31 the same day with all 64 Democrats YES. Individual line-items not separately pulled from the JS-rendered LIS pages, but these were strict-party-line recorded floor votes. Notable campaign cross-current (left UNSCORED, no vote): she favored shortening Virginia's early-voting window 45->30 days, and self-identifies as a gun owner while backing background checks/storage. Treat as a thin but real evidence record.

0.note: HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (second reference), passed the House 64-34 on Jan 14, 2026 with all 64 Democrats voting YES; Anderson, a seated Democrat, is within that strict-party-line bloc — does not affirm personhood from conception.

HJ3, the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment (second reference), passed the House 67-31 on Jan 14, 2026 with all 64 Democrats voting YES; Anderson is within that strict-party-line bloc — does not affirm man-woman marriage.

1.note: Within the all-Democrat YES bloc codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3 second reference).

profile.religion: Roman Catholic; member of St. John Neumann Catholic Church, Powhatan, VA
profile.background: Robert Lee Ware Jr. (R), VA House of Delegates District 72 (Powhatan/Goochland area). One of the chamber's most senior members — in office since 1998 (formerly HD-65 before 2021 redistricting); re-elected Nov 4, 2025 (NOT retiring). Career history/government teacher and former academic dean; B.A. History & Literature, Wheaton College (IL), 1974. Former Chairman of House Finance.

lee-ware.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-72 (senior member since 1998). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent conservative HARD record. SANCTITY: voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1; 2025 first ref party-line 51-48 all R no; 2026 second ref 64-34 all 34 R no) and is chief patron of HB2605 (2025), the Medical Conscience Protection Act. MARRIAGE: on HJ9 (2025 marriage repeal amendment, House 58-35) Cardinal News named the 7 crossover Republicans (Bloxom, Coyner, Garrett, Green, McNamara, Obenshain, Wachsmann) — Ware was NOT among them; he voted with the 35 Republicans opposing, and again opposed at the 2026 second reference (67-31). CHRISTIAN LIBERTY: chief patron of the Medical Conscience Protection Act (HB2605) — conscience exemption for health-care providers. ELECTION INTEGRITY: chief patron of HB1444 (2023), photo voter ID requiring repeal of the ID-statement affidavit option. SELF-DEFENSE: voted against the 2020 assault-weapons/magazine ban (HB961, all R opposed) and with GOP against the 2026 gun-control package. No cross-party deviations — held the conservative line on both the abortion and marriage amendments. Family/child, public-justice, immigration, CBDC/balanced-budget, and federal-overreach (HB8 Article V item unverified) returned no clean confirmable record this pass and were left null.

0.note: Voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1; 2025 first ref party-line 51-48 all Republicans no; 2026 second ref 64-34 all 34 R no) and is chief patron of HB2605 (2025), the Medical Conscience Protection Act — consistent pro-life record.

On the HJ9 (2025) marriage repeal amendment (House 58-35), Cardinal News named the 7 crossover Republicans; Ware was NOT among them — he voted with the 35 Republicans opposing, and opposed again at the 2026 second reference (67-31). Affirms man-woman marriage.

1.note: Voted to keep Virginia's constitutional same-sex-marriage ban (NO on the HJ9/HJ3 repeal amendment) — opposes same-sex marriage in law.

Chief patron of HB2605 (2025), the Medical Conscience Protection Act — shields medical providers from being compelled to participate in procedures (incl. abortion) that violate conscience. Direct support for conscience exemptions for medical professionals.
Chief patron of HB1444 (2023), which would require photo ID to vote and repeal the ID-statement affidavit option — supports photo voter ID.

Voted against the 2020 assault-weapons/magazine ban (HB961, House 51-48 with all Republicans opposed) and with the GOP against the 2026 gun-control package — opposes assault-weapons bans and magazine limits.
